en: Optical radiation hazard assessment is required for activities with possible exposure and must consider radiation type, duration, and workplace conditions.\nOrdinance on Optical Radiation, Section 1(1), 5
Exposure limit compliance is required and must ensure artificial optical radiation limits are not exceeded for incoherent or laser sources.\nOrdinance on Optical Radiation, Section 3(1)

Workplace design considerations must address separation, shielding, and layout controls. Exposure reduction measures must be implemented and must consider alternative equipment and technical controls.\nOrdinance on Optical Radiation, Section 5(4)(1-2), 7(1)
Control Measures (Engineering, Administrative, and/or Personal Protective Equipment)
Employee information on radiation risks is required and must address hazards and protective measures.\nOrdinance on Optical Radiation, Section 6(1)
